sroi_caveat: TrueCase SDG mapping is directional signal only. It is not an investment-grade SROI assessment. Organisations requiring formal SROI should commission a full assessment using the UK Cabinet Office SROI methodology.

primary_sdg.name: Reduced Inequalities
primary_sdg.target_text: By 2030, empower and promote the social, economic and political inclusion of all, irrespective of age, sex, disability, race, ethnicity, origin, religion or economic or other status
primary_sdg.connection: AI credit and financial decisions that are explainable and contestable support equal access to financial services regardless of background. Without a decision audit trail and human review, automated financial decisions risk perpetuating the exclusions they were designed to remove.

secondary_sdg.name: Decent Work and Economic Growth
secondary_sdg.target_text: Strengthen the capacity of domestic financial institutions to encourage and expand access to banking, insurance and financial services for all
secondary_sdg.connection: Governed AI in financial services can broaden access to credit and insurance — but only when governance controls prevent the automation of historical exclusions.

mappings.negative_condition: Without a Human Review Requirement and Decision Audit Trail, this system risks automating financial exclusion at scale — directly contradicting SDG 10's intent.

primary_sdg.name: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
primary_sdg.target_text: Ensure public access to information and protect fundamental freedoms, in accordance with national legislation and international agreements
primary_sdg.connection: AI content moderation systems that operate transparently and with human oversight support public access to information and protection of freedom of expression. Ungoverned automated moderation risks suppressing legitimate speech at scale.

secondary_sdg.name: Reduced Inequalities
secondary_sdg.target_text: By 2030, empower and promote the social, economic and political inclusion of all, irrespective of age, sex, disability, race, ethnicity, origin, religion or economic or other status
secondary_sdg.connection: Content moderation AI that disproportionately removes content from marginalised communities without explainable, contestable decisions undermines social and political inclusion.

mappings.negative_condition: Without Human Review and a Decision Audit Trail, automated content decisions risk suppressing speech and undermining the public interest obligations SDG 16 protects.

primary_sdg.name: Good Health and Well-Being
primary_sdg.target_text: Achieve universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality essential health-care services and access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all
primary_sdg.connection: AI clinical decision support that includes documented human review and a decision audit trail supports safe, equitable health coverage.

secondary_sdg.name: Reduced Inequalities
secondary_sdg.target_text: Ensure equal opportunity and reduce inequalities of outcome, including by eliminating discriminatory laws, policies and practices
secondary_sdg.connection: Healthcare AI with documented system performance is more likely to perform equitably across patient populations.

mappings.negative_condition: Without Human Review, a Decision Audit Trail, and AI System Performance Documentation, this system risks encoding demographic biases into care decisions — directly contradicting SDG 3 and SDG 10.

primary_sdg.name: Decent Work and Economic Growth
primary_sdg.target_text: By 2030, achieve full and productive employment and decent work for all women and men, including for young people and persons with disabilities, and equal pay for work of equal value
primary_sdg.connection: AI hiring and assessment tools with documented system performance, human review, and a decision audit trail support equal employment opportunity.

secondary_sdg.name: Reduced Inequalities
secondary_sdg.target_text: By 2030, empower and promote the social, economic and political inclusion of all, irrespective of age, sex, disability, race, ethnicity, origin, religion or economic or other status
secondary_sdg.connection: Explainable, contestable AI employment decisions reduce the risk that automated systems perpetuate exclusion of protected groups from economic participation.

mappings.negative_condition: Without Human Review, a Decision Audit Trail, and AI System Performance Documentation, this system risks encoding historical hiring discrimination — directly contradicting SDG 8 and SDG 10.

primary_sdg.name: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
primary_sdg.target_text: Develop effective, accountable and transparent institutions at all levels
primary_sdg.connection: AI systems in public services with documented governance support accountable and transparent public institutions. Ungoverned public sector AI has been directly linked to unlawful automated decisions in UK local authorities.

secondary_sdg.name: Reduced Inequalities
secondary_sdg.target_text: By 2030, empower and promote the social, economic and political inclusion of all, irrespective of age, sex, disability, race, ethnicity, origin, religion or economic or other status
secondary_sdg.connection: Public sector AI that affects individuals' access to services must be explainable and contestable to support equal treatment under public law.

mappings.negative_condition: Without Human Review, a Decision Audit Trail, and a Regulatory Compliance Map, this system risks unlawful automated public decisions — directly contradicting SDG 16.

primary_sdg.name: Reduced Inequalities
primary_sdg.target_text: Adopt policies, especially fiscal, wage and social protection policies, and progressively achieve greater equality
primary_sdg.connection: AI pricing systems with output limit controls prevent price surges from disproportionately excluding lower-income buyers. The CMA's 2024 investigation established uncapped AI pricing as a consumer harm concern.

secondary_sdg.name: Decent Work and Economic Growth
secondary_sdg.target_text: Achieve higher levels of economic productivity through diversification, technological upgrading and innovation
secondary_sdg.connection: Governed AI pricing supports sustainable economic activity in retail, e-commerce, and live events sectors.

mappings.negative_condition: Without an Output Limit Control and Regulatory Compliance Map, this system risks triggering CMA enforcement action — undermining rather than supporting SDG 10.

primary_sdg.name: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
primary_sdg.target_text: Ensure public access to information and protect fundamental freedoms, in accordance with national legislation and international agreements
primary_sdg.connection: AI systems on technology platforms that affect what individuals see or access require governance controls to protect public access to information and freedom of expression at scale.

secondary_sdg.name: Reduced Inequalities
secondary_sdg.target_text: By 2030, empower and promote the social, economic and political inclusion of all, irrespective of age, sex, disability, race, ethnicity, origin, religion or economic or other status
secondary_sdg.connection: Platform AI with human review and decision audit trails reduces the risk of algorithmic exclusion of marginalised communities.

mappings.negative_condition: Without Output Limit Control, Human Review, and a Decision Audit Trail, platform AI risks suppressing access to information at scale — directly contradicting SDG 16.

primary_sdg.name: Reduced Inequalities
primary_sdg.target_text: By 2030, empower and promote the social, economic and political inclusion of all, irrespective of age, sex, disability, race, ethnicity, origin, religion or economic or other status
primary_sdg.connection: AI systems that make decisions affecting individuals with documented governance reduce the risk that automation perpetuates or amplifies existing inequalities.

secondary_sdg.name: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
secondary_sdg.target_text: Develop effective, accountable and transparent institutions at all levels
secondary_sdg.connection: Governed AI decision-making supports the development of accountable institutions and processes.

mappings.negative_condition: Without Human Review and a Decision Audit Trail, this system risks automating decisions that harm individuals without recourse — contradicting SDG 10 and SDG 16.

primary_sdg.name: Decent Work and Economic Growth
primary_sdg.target_text: Strengthen the capacity of domestic financial institutions to encourage and expand access to banking, insurance and financial services for all
primary_sdg.connection: AI risk and market analysis tools with documented governance support stronger, more reliable financial institutions and broader market access.

secondary_sdg.name: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
secondary_sdg.target_text: Enhance scientific research, upgrade the technological capabilities of industrial sectors in all countries
secondary_sdg.connection: Governed AI investment in financial services supports sustainable technological innovation in a regulated sector.

mappings.negative_condition: Without an Output Limit Control and Regulatory Compliance Map, this system has unquantified regulatory exposure that undermines its projected contribution to SDG 8.

primary_sdg.name: Sustainable Cities and Communities
primary_sdg.target_text: By 2030, enhance inclusive and sustainable urbanization and capacity for participatory, integrated and sustainable human settlement planning and management in all countries
primary_sdg.connection: AI land and property analysis tools with documented governance support transparent, evidence-based property investment decisions that contribute to sustainable urban planning.

secondary_sdg.name: Reduced Inequalities
secondary_sdg.target_text: By 2030, empower and promote the social, economic and political inclusion of all, irrespective of age, sex, disability, race, ethnicity, origin, religion or economic or other status
secondary_sdg.connection: AI property tools with documented system performance help mitigate the risk of concentrating investment in already-advantaged areas.

mappings.negative_condition: Without AI System Performance Documentation and Output Limit Controls, this system risks amplifying spatial inequality — contradicting SDG 11 and SDG 10.

primary_sdg.name: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
primary_sdg.target_text: Ensure public access to information and protect fundamental freedoms, in accordance with national legislation and international agreements
primary_sdg.connection: AI editorial and distribution tools in broadcasting with documented governance support the public interest obligations that underpin free and fair media.

secondary_sdg.name: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
secondary_sdg.target_text: Enhance scientific research, upgrade the technological capabilities of industrial sectors
secondary_sdg.connection: Governed AI investment in media production and distribution supports sustainable innovation in the creative industries.

mappings.negative_condition: Without a Regulatory Compliance Map and Output Limit Control, this system has unaddressed Online Safety Act and Ofcom obligations that undermine its SDG 16 claim.

primary_sdg.name: Decent Work and Economic Growth
primary_sdg.target_text: Achieve higher levels of economic productivity through diversification, technological upgrading and innovation
primary_sdg.connection: AI market tools in retail with documented governance support productive economic activity and sustainable commercial innovation.

secondary_sdg.name: Reduced Inequalities
secondary_sdg.target_text: Adopt policies, especially fiscal, wage and social protection policies, and progressively achieve greater equality
secondary_sdg.connection: Market-level AI pricing tools with output limit controls prevent algorithmic price coordination that could harm consumers and small businesses.

mappings.negative_condition: Without Output Limit Control and Regulatory Compliance Map, this system has CMA exposure that undermines its SDG 8 contribution.

primary_sdg.name: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
primary_sdg.target_text: Enhance scientific research, upgrade the technological capabilities of industrial sectors in all countries
primary_sdg.connection: AI systems supporting organisational decisions with documented governance contribute to sustainable technological innovation.

secondary_sdg.name: Decent Work and Economic Growth
secondary_sdg.target_text: Achieve higher levels of economic productivity through diversification, technological upgrading and innovation
secondary_sdg.connection: Governed AI investment in organisational decision-making supports productive economic activity and responsible innovation.

mappings.negative_condition: Without AI System Performance Documentation and a Regulatory Compliance Map, projected SDG contributions are not supported by the governance conditions required to deliver them.

primary_sdg.name: Decent Work and Economic Growth
primary_sdg.target_text: Achieve higher levels of economic productivity through diversification, technological upgrading and innovation, including through a focus on high value added and labour-intensive sectors
primary_sdg.connection: Internal AI automation with documented system performance supports productive economic activity without the risk of undocumented performance degradation offsetting projected gains.

secondary_sdg.name: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
secondary_sdg.target_text: Enhance scientific research, upgrade the technological capabilities of industrial sectors in all countries
secondary_sdg.connection: Internal AI tools with governed deployment contribute to sustainable technological capability-building.

mappings.negative_condition: Without AI System Performance Documentation, projected productivity gains from this system have no documented basis for persistence over the ROI projection period.

primary_sdg.name: Decent Work and Economic Growth
primary_sdg.target_text: Achieve higher levels of economic productivity through diversification, technological upgrading and innovation
primary_sdg.connection: Process automation AI with documented governance supports sustainable productivity improvement without perpetuating inefficiencies from the processes being automated.

secondary_sdg.name: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
secondary_sdg.target_text: By 2030, upgrade infrastructure and retrofit industries to make them sustainable, with increased resource-use efficiency and greater adoption of clean and environmentally sound technologies
secondary_sdg.connection: Governed AI process automation contributes to the sustainable modernisation of organisational processes.

mappings.negative_condition: Without AI System Performance Documentation, this automation may perpetuate the errors or inefficiencies of the process it replaces — with no mechanism to detect this until outcomes degrade.
